title: Kaldereta Stuffed Cabbage Rolls
description: A twist on the classic Filipino dish, where cabbage leaves are stuffed with a rich meat mixture and simmered in a flavorful sauce.
instructions: In a large pan over medium heat, cook the ground beef until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic to the pan and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Stir in the Filipino soy sauce, grassy tomato sauce, and dense beef broth. Bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the shredded savoy cabbage to the pan and cook, stirring occasionally, until it has broken down and the mixture has thickened.
Meanwhile, blanch the dense cabbage leaves in boiling water until they are pliable. Remove them from the water and let them cool.
To assemble the cabbage rolls, lay a cabbage leaf flat on a work surface and place a tablespoon of the meat mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the stem end of the leaf over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a tight cylinder. Repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
Place the cabbage rolls seam-side down in the pan and spoon some of the sauce over them.
Simmer the cabbage rolls, covered, for 20-25 minutes, until they are tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season the cabbage rolls with salt and pepper to taste, then serve hot.
